display.label: Vital Stats — Natality (Live Births / ABR / RABR)

notes: Vital Stats Natality. Province/HUC granularity only — 4 real rows (Negros Occidental, Negros Oriental, Siquijor, City of Bacolod HUC); NIR region rollup (row 2) skipped via data_start_row:1. location_column:2 — col 1 ('Region') is a constant 'NIR' literal, the real name is col 2 (FLAG 5). Only Q1 carries data (Q3/Q4 blank but same 17-col layout, safe to map). Q2 EXCLUDED: it is structurally missing the ABR<10 column (16 cols vs 17), shifting every field left — the parser uses one shared columns array so mapping Q2 here would silently mis-read; DOH-blocked pending the missing-column fix (FLAG 1). Annual sheet is a #REF!-broken SUM rollup — NOT ingested; annual/quarterly views aggregate stored data. ABR 10-14 / 15-19 rate columns are 'per 1,000' (headers wrongly say '%', FLAG 2) — stored already-multiplied (ADR-023). RABR 15-19 is a true percentage (count/deliveries, no x1000). RABR 10-14 has no denominator in the template (FLAG 3) — stored as a bare count. LB_TOTAL is the shared MMR denominator per the file's own change_log (FLAG 4). Deferred DQC (need D4 sum-of-parts rule): ABR_10_14+ABR_15_19 <= LB_TOTAL; RABR_15_19 <= DELIVERIES_15_19. Minor-privacy sensitivity of adolescent-birth data is an open policy question for Joseph (not in current Tier-1 sensitive list).
